preamble: The fortition of a voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into a voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is observed in various Arabic dialects, notably Sudanese Arabic. This shift often involves the strengthening of a weakened /q/ or a natural progression from a fricative to a stop in specific phonological contexts.

examples.note: Strengthening of original /q/ or its weakened variants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
